title: Boba Fett: Pursuit
content:

'''''Boba Fett: Pursuit''''' is the sixth and final in a series of young-reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] during the years between [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].

==Publisher's summary==
'''A father's death. A son's revenge.'''

''Boba Fett is emerging as one of the most skilled bounty hunters in the galaxy. After surviving his deadly battle with the fearsome General Grievous, there's one bounty he wants more than any other: Mace Windu, the Jedi who killed his father.''

''When fate and Anakin Skywalker bring Boba Fett to the city-planet of Coruscant, Boba plunges into the Coruscant underworld and arms himself for the greatest confrontation of his young life.''

''It's the bounty hunter vs. the Jedi as Boba Fett takes his one and only chance at revenge.''

==Plot summary==
A twelve-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] is inside a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] stronghold on [[Xagobah/Legends|Xagobah]]. Fett lies on the ground, paralyzed by a [[Poison/Legends|toxin]] used to escape a near-[[Death/Legends|death]] experience against [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. [[Gender/Legends|He]] is taken to a blast furnace to be destroyed, but escapes when his paralysis subsides and he destroys two [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. He leaves the stronghold and reaches [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. Once in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] he spots his primary [[Wat Tambor/Legends|target]], and attacks [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Tambor's]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]]. After damaging the vulnerable vessel, he has to break off the attack because of [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]], who nearly destroys him save for [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] intervening in [[Time/Legends|time]].

Skywalker forces Fett to land on a nearby [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. Exchanging greetings, Skywalker begins repairing ''Slave I'' while Fett repairs his armor. Once finished, Skywalker moves to detain Fett, but a giant [[Exogorth/Legends|space slug]] incapacitates Anakin, leaving Fett to destroy the behemoth. He shoots the slug several times in the head, causing it to whimper back into cover, and Skywalker agrees not to arrest Fett, but tells him to go to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] as Fett revealed he had information vital to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

On arriving in Coruscant airspace, he is escorted by [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin]] and [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. Inside, Fett makes his way to the visiting quarters, passing [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] (who he blames for his [[Jango Fett/Legends|father's]] death) on the way. In his quarters, Fett contacts [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] contact on Coruscant, [[Hat Lo/Legends|Hat Lo]]. Boba waits at a landing dock for [[Elan Sel'Sabagno/Legends|Elan Sel'Sabagno]], who flies him to a bar that Hat Lo owns where Boba collects various weapons before returning to the Temple with Elan. Once inside, he races to Windu's quarters only to be too late. He breaks through a window and flies to the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. He breaks into [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] quarters and finds Windu waiting for the Chancellor. They engage in a fight, with Windu eventually taking out most of Fett's weapons. The fight ends in stalemate with each about to strike when Palpatine arrives and scolds Windu.

Boba tells Palpatine about the trap that the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]] has become because of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. Palpatine acknowledges that he knows and reveals his intentions indirectly to Boba. The Chancellor gives Boba a [[Credit cube|cube of credits]] to keep him quiet. Boba returns to ''Slave I'' to contemplate his future, finally shedding his past.

==Continuity==
The events of the novel contradict the story of ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''. In the book, Mace Windu doesn't know about the existence of Boba Fett and his relationship to Jango, while in the TV show he does. Furthermore, in the series Boba attempts to get revenge on Mace Windu by infiltrating his Jedi cruiser and when made the ship crash he used Jango Fett's [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] by placing a motion-sensitive [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]] in it. Boba at this time had no [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]. Instead, he wore a dark gray jumpsuit with a brown belt with a twin [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] holster and white kneepads with boots that seemed very similar to the boots his father had worn.

On the front cover, Anakin Skywalker's appearance is similar to how he looks in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']]. His image is flipped, causing his hair to be parted on the wrong side, his scar to be on the left side of his face instead of the right and his [[Glove/Legends|glove]] to be on the incorrect [[Limb/Legends|hand]].

Asajj Ventress and her [[Ginivex-class starfighter/Legends|Fanblade starfighter]] are also on the cover. She appears in a dogfight against Boba, despite the fact that she apparently deserted the Separatist cause after the [[Battle of Boz Pity (Clone Wars)|Battle of Boz Pity]] only one [[Standard month/Legends|month]] earlier.

Two [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] are shown on the cover, but these vessels do not feature in the story.

Boba's helmet on the front cover bears a dent it will not receive until after the events of this novel in a fight against [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader.

[[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] appears in this book, prior to his becoming a [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]].

Boba Fett confronts Mace Windu to avenge his father's death. Many fans had expressed their wishes to see such a confrontation. Fett, then a hot-headed [[Youngling/Legends|child]], was able to match the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], but the fight was ended before either was killed. Darth Vader's future impression of Boba Fett as a highly capable and reliable bounty hunter may have been shaped by this event.

Boba Fett re-paints his father's armor in the [[Color/Legends|green]] and red livery that will later achieve infamy.

On the spines of later editions of this title, the word "Wars" was accidentally omitted from the name ''[[Star Wars]]''.

This book incorrectly states Boba Fett had never been to Coruscant before, but he in fact had in ''[[Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive]]''.

The book incorrectly describes Jedi Luminara Unduli as having dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and bronze skin, when she has bright blue eyes and olive green skin.

At the end of the story, Boba Fett entertains the notion of visiting the [[Regional Sector Number Four's All-Human Free-For-All]] on [[Jubilar/Legends|Jubilar]], suggesting that the events at the start of the short story "[[The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett]]" happen immediately thereafter. However, according to the timeline, this story takes place at least six years earlier. Of course it would be possible for Fett to visit the games before that.
